title: Resolve announce coordinate longitude range encoding
description: Final review of project-LICHEN-zte2.9.1 identified that spec/05-routing.md app_data Type=0x01 encodes both lat and lon as signed int24 at 1e-5 degree resolution, which only covers +/-83.88607 degrees. That cannot represent longitudes for much of the world (for example US west coast). A non-conforming producer that truncates longitude low 24 bits can decode to a plausible but wrong in-range longitude, and the decoder cannot detect this from the 7-byte field. Decide and update the protocol/test vectors/Python/C decoders: use a wider longitude field, lower resolution, biased encoding, or explicitly constrain/validate coordinate source domains. Add cross-implementation vectors and simulator tests.
notes:
Resolved announce coordinate longitude encoding as a no-hardware protocol/reference implementation slice. Updated spec/05-routing.md Type=0x01 app_data from signed int24 1e-5 lat/lon to signed big-endian e7 int32 lat/lon: type(1) + lat_e7(4) + lon_e7(4), full latitude +/-90 and longitude +/-180. Updated Python announce coords encoder/decoder with pre-rounding input range checks and decode-side raw e7 range rejects. Updated Python Meshtastic translator to use canonical Type=0x01 e7 app_data instead of CBOR for Position<->announce coordinate translation. Updated Zephyr gateway announce decoder to consume 9-byte e7 app_data and validate coordinate bounds.

Added canonical test/vectors/announce_coords.json with zero, Seattle west-longitude, positive limit, and negative limit vectors. Updated schema to enforce ^01[0-9a-f]{16}$, README docs, generator, and Python vector drift tests. Updated Python coordinate, GPSR simulator, topology, and Meshtastic translator tests. Updated Zephyr meshtastic_gateway_adapter tests to mirror the canonical vector payloads and to reject malformed raw e7 values including +/-900000001, +/-1800000001, INT32_MAX, and INT32_MIN.

Reviews: initial final reviews found Python decode accepted out-of-range e7, encode rounded slightly out-of-range floats onto the boundary, schema did not enforce the exact 9-byte Type=0x01 shape, Meshtastic translator still used CBOR app_data, and C tests lacked canonical vector and malformed e7 coverage. Fixed all P0/P1/P2 findings. Final reviews by Herschel the 9th and Raman the 9th found no P0/P1/P2 issues in the coordinate changes. Raman noted the pre-existing untracked Renode script has absolute paths if committed; it remains uncommitted and outside this Bead.

Validation: git diff --check passed. python3 -m py_compile python/src/lichen/announce/coords.py python/src/lichen/interface/meshtastic/translate.py python/tests/announce/test_coords.py python/tests/test_vectors.py python/tests/sim/test_gpsr_sim.py python/tests/interface/test_meshtastic_translate.py passed. PYTHONPATH=python/src python3 test/vectors/generate.py regenerated vectors with no drift in existing files and wrote 4 announce coordinate vectors. uv run pytest python/tests/announce/test_coords.py python/tests/test_vectors.py python/tests/sim/test_gpsr_sim.py python/tests/sim/test_topology_scenarios.py python/tests/interface/test_meshtastic_translate.py -q passed 215/215. EC2 us-east-2a instance i-0a3288920ad0ba530 using EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58, isolated worktree /mnt/lichen-zephyr/work/project-LICHEN-qbgj: west twister -T lichen/tests/meshtastic_gateway_adapter -p native_sim -p qemu_x86 --inline-logs --outdir twister-out-qbgj passed 2/2 executed configs, 0 failed, 0 errored, 2 skipped, 134 test cases, no warnings, 29.41s. Size output: native_sim zephyr.elf text=300092 data=7895 bss=76190 dec=384177; native_sim zephyr.exe text=319912 data=8974 bss=77088 dec=405974; qemu_x86 zephyr.elf text=256468 data=5836 bss=155956 dec=418260.
close_reason: Completed protocol/reference implementation update to 9-byte signed e7 announce coordinates with vectors, Python/C tests, reviews, and EC2 Twister validation.
